title: helia pumila
intro: Helia pumila, also known as creeping bellflower, is a low-growing, flowering plant native to Europe and Asia, characterized by its blue-purple bell-shaped flowers and heart-shaped leaves. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help to reduce inflammation and prevent infection. The health benefits of Helia pumila include its ability to alleviate respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its potential to reduce anxiety and promote relaxation. Herbal preparations of Helia pumila can be made into teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties and health benefits.
